Crackles/wheezing/diminished breath sounds: Assess SpO2, position upright, notify provider
Irregular rhythm, new murmur, absent pulses: Obtain 12-lead ECG, assess perfusion
Rigid/distended abdomen, absent bowel sounds: May indicate obstruction or peritonitis: NPO, notify provider
New weakness, facial droop, speech changes: Activate stroke protocol (FAST) immediately

quiz.question: When performing an abdominal assessment, what is the correct sequence of techniques?
quiz.rationale: For the abdomen ONLY, the correct order is Inspect → Auscultate → Percuss → Palpate. Auscultation is performed BEFORE percussion and palpation because touching or tapping the abdomen can stimulate peristalsis and alter bowel sounds, producing false results. For all other body systems, the standard sequence is Inspect → Palpate → Percuss → Auscultate.

head-to-toe-rpn.title: Başdan Aşağı Değerlendirme (/)
head-to-toe-rpn.pathophysiology:
Başdan aşağı değerlendirme, her vardiyanın başında, hasta kabulünde ve klinik durum değiştiğinde gerçekleştirilen sistematik, kafa ayak (cephalocaudal) fizik muayenedir. Sırasıyla kullanılan dört teknik şunlardır: İnceleme (görsel gözlem), Palpasyon (dokunma), Perküsyon (yoğunluğu değerlendirmek için vurma) ve Auskültasyon (stetoskopla dinleme). Abdominal değerlendirme için sıra değişir: İncele → Auskültasyon → Perküsyon → Palpasyon (bağırsak seslerini değiştirmemek için palpasyon/perküsyondan önce auskültasyon yapın).

Değerlendirme sistematik bir sırayı takip eder:
1. Genel Anket: Görünüm, duruş, hijyen, LOC, sıkıntı seviyesi
2. Baş: Kafa simetrisi, saç dağılımı, saç derisi lezyonları, yüz simetrisi
3. Gözler: PERRLA (Pupiller Eşit Yuvarlak Işığa ve Konaklamaya Reaktif), görme keskinliği, konjonktiva rengi, sklera
4. Kulaklar: Dış kulak muayenesi, işitme yanıtı, eğitimli ise timpanik membran
5. Burun ve Sinüsler: Açıklık, akıntı, sinüslerde hassasiyet
6. Ağız ve Boğaz: Mukozal zarlar, diş durumu, dil, bademcikler, kusma refleksi
7. Boyun: ROM, JVD, tiroid, lenf düğümleri, trakeal pozisyon (orta hat)
8. Göğüs/Respiratuvar: Solunum hızı/deseni/çabası, akciğer sesleri tüm alanlarda iki taraflı
9. Kardiyovasküler: Kalp sesleri (S1, S2), hız/ritim, periferik nabızlar, kapiller dolum, ödem
10. Karın: İncele → Auskültasyon → Perküsyon → Palpasyon, bağırsak sesleri tüm 4 kadranda
11. Kas-İskelet: ROM, güç, simetri, yürüyüş
12. Nörolojik: LOC, yönelim (kişi/yer/zaman/durum), kranial sinirler, his, refleksler
13. İntegratif: Cilt rengi, turgor, nem, lezyonlar, basınç noktaları
14. Periferik Vasküler: Nabızlar, sıcaklık, renk, ödem, Homans işareti (tarihsel)

diagnostics: Temperature measurement (oral, tympanic, temporal, rectal, axillary)
Pulse assessment (rate, rhythm, quality)
Respiratory assessment (rate, depth, pattern)
Blood pressure measurement (correct cuff size, positioned at heart level)
Oxygen saturation via pulse oximetry
management: Report abnormal vital signs to RN/charge nurse immediately
Retake vital signs if values seem inconsistent with patient status
Monitor trending of vital signs over time
Implement standing orders for out-of-range values if applicable
nursingActions: Measure vital signs at prescribed intervals and PRN
Use correct equipment and technique for each measurement
Report abnormal values immediately using SBAR
Trend vital signs to identify patterns of deterioration
Document all values accurately with time of measurement
Assess pain as the 5th vital sign using appropriate scale
clinicalPearls: Count respirations without the patient knowing: if they are aware, they may consciously alter their breathing pattern. Count while appearing to take the pulse (keep fingers on wrist).

Orthostatic (postural) vital signs: Measure BP and HR lying → sitting → standing (wait 1-3 min between positions). A drop of ≥20 mmHg systolic, ≥10 mmHg diastolic, or HR increase ≥20 bpm is positive for orthostatic hypotension.
Widening pulse pressure (systolic rises, diastolic falls) with bradycardia = Cushing's triad: late sign of increased ICP. This is a medical emergency.
Never delegate the first assessment of vital signs to unlicensed personnel: the RPN/RN must establish the baseline.
Pain is subjective: the patient's self-report is the most reliable indicator. Use age-appropriate scales: NRS (0-10) for adults, Wong-Baker FACES for children, FLACC for nonverbal patients.

medications.name: Sphygmomanometer
medications.action: Measures arterial blood pressure using an inflatable cuff to occlude the brachial artery and a manometer to measure the pressure. Korotkoff sounds are auscultated: Phase 1 (first sound = systolic), Phase 5 (disappearance = diastolic). Automated devices use oscillometry
medications.sideEffects: Incorrect cuff size gives inaccurate readings: too small = falsely high, too large = falsely low
medications.contra: Do not measure BP on arm with: IV infusion, AV fistula, lymphedema, mastectomy side, or recent injury
medications.pearl: Cuff bladder should cover 80% of arm circumference. Patient should rest 5 minutes before measurement, feet flat on floor, arm supported at heart level, no talking. Take in both arms on first visit: a difference >10 mmHg may indicate coarctation of aorta or subclavian stenosis.

left: Temperature: Oral 36.5-37.5°C, Axillary subtract 0.3-0.6°C, Rectal add 0.3-0.6°C, Tympanic comparable to core
Pulse: Rate 60-100 bpm, Regular rhythm, 2+ amplitude, Radial most common site
Respirations: Rate 12-20/min, Regular rhythm, Unlabored, No accessory muscle use
BP: <120/80 normal, Proper cuff size (bladder 80% of arm circumference), Support arm at heart level
SpO2: 95-100% normal, >92% acceptable for COPD patients, Apply to warm, well-perfused digit
right: Fever >38.3°C: Tachycardia, diaphoresis, may indicate infection: obtain cultures before antibiotics
Tachycardia >100: Pain, fever, dehydration, anxiety, hemorrhage, hypoxia: investigate the cause
Bradycardia <60: May be normal in athletes, or indicate medication effect (beta-blockers), increased ICP, hypothyroidism
Tachypnea >20: Early sign of respiratory distress, metabolic acidosis, pain, anxiety: assess SpO2
Hypotension <90/60: Assess for hemorrhage, sepsis, dehydration, medication effect: position supine, IV fluids
SpO2 <90%: Respiratory failure: apply O2, elevate HOB, prepare for escalation

quiz.question: A nurse obtains a BP reading of 180/60 mmHg with a heart rate of 52 bpm on a patient with a head injury. What does this indicate?
options: Cushing's triad: late sign of increased ICP
Hypovolemic shock requiring immediate fluid resuscitation
quiz.rationale: Cushing's triad consists of hypertension (specifically WIDENING pulse pressure: 180-60=120 mmHg), bradycardia, and irregular respirations. This is a LATE and ominous sign of increased intracranial pressure (ICP) indicating brainstem compression. The widening pulse pressure (normally 30-40 mmHg) occurs because the body attempts to maintain cerebral perfusion by raising systolic pressure. Bradycardia is a vagal response to the rising pressure. This is a medical emergency requiring immediate intervention.

vital-signs-assessment.title: Hayati Belirtiler: Tam Değerlendirme Rehberi
vital-signs-assessment.pathophysiology:
Hayati belirtiler, vücudun temel fizyolojik işlevlerini ve homeostatik mekanizmalarını yansıtır. Hemşireler tarafından toplanan en temel değerlendirme verileridir.

Sıcaklık: Hipotalamus tarafından düzenlenir. Vücut sıcaklığı, ısı üretimi (metabolizma, kas aktivitesi, tiroksin gibi hormonlar) ve ısı kaybı (ışınım, iletim, konveksiyon, buharlaşma) arasındaki dengeyi yansıtır. Normal: 36.5-37.5°C (97.7-99.5°F).

Nabız: Her kalp atışı, arteriyel sistemde bir basınç dalgası gönderir. Hız, ritim ve amplitüd kalp fonksiyonu, hacim durumu ve otonom tonunu yansıtır. Normal yetişkin: 60-100 bpm.

Solunum: Medüller solunum merkezi tarafından kontrol edilir, CO2, O2 ve pH'yi algılayan merkezi ve periferik kemoreseptörlerden girdi alır. Hız, derinlik, ritim ve çaba solunum ve metabolik durumu yansıtır. Normal yetişkin: 12-20 nefes/dk.

Kan Basıncı: Dolaşan kanın damar duvarlarına uyguladığı kuvvet. Sistolik = ventriküler kasılma; Diastolik = ventriküler gevşeme. Kardiyak debi (HR × darbe hacmi) ve sistemik vasküler direnç tarafından belirlenir. Normal yetişkin: <120/80 mmHg.

Ağrı (5. hayati belirti): Nociceptör aktivasyonu, iltihaplayıcı medyatörler ve CNS işleme tarafından etkilenen öznel bir deneyimdir. Her zaman geleneksel hayati belirtilerle birlikte değerlendirilir.

Nabız Oksimetresi: SpO2, ışık emilimi kullanarak oksijenle doymuş hemoglobin yüzdesini ölçer. Normal: 95-100%.

riskFactors: History of substance abuse (may underreport or overreport)
diagnostics: Numeric Pain Scale (0-10) for adults who can self-report
Wong-Baker FACES scale for children and non-verbal patients
FLACC scale for infants and young children
Behavioral assessment for non-verbal/cognitively impaired patients
Vital signs (pain may cause tachycardia, hypertension)
management: Administer prescribed analgesics as ordered
Implement non-pharmacological pain interventions (positioning, ice/heat, distraction)
Reassess pain 30 minutes after IV medication, 60 minutes after oral medication
Report uncontrolled pain to RN/physician
Document pain level before and after interventions
nursingActions: Assess pain using appropriate validated scale
Believe patient's self-report of pain (gold standard)
Administer prescribed analgesics and reassess effectiveness
Use non-pharmacological interventions as adjunct to medication
Document pain assessment, interventions, and reassessment
Report inadequate pain relief to provider for order adjustment
clinicalPearls: The patient's self-report is always the GOLD STANDARD for pain assessment: believe the patient. Pain is subjective and cannot be objectively measured.
Reassess pain after every intervention: 30 min after IV medication, 60 min after oral medication, 15-20 min after repositioning or non-pharmacological intervention.

Multimodal analgesia is best practice: Combine non-opioid (acetaminophen, NSAIDs), opioid (if needed), and non-pharmacological methods (positioning, ice/heat, distraction, relaxation) for synergistic effect and reduced opioid requirements.
For nonverbal patients (intubated, cognitively impaired, neonates), use behavioral pain tools (FLACC, CPOT) rather than assuming no pain. Absence of self-report does NOT equal absence of pain.
Document: Pain intensity (using standardized scale), location, quality (sharp/dull/burning/aching), onset, duration, aggravating/alleviating factors, impact on function, and patient's pain goal.

medications.name: Acetaminophen (Tylenol)
medications.action: Inhibits prostaglandin synthesis in the CNS (not peripherally: therefore NO anti-inflammatory effect). Provides mild-moderate pain relief and fever reduction
medications.sideEffects: Hepatotoxicity at high doses: max 4g/day in healthy adults, 2g/day in liver disease or elderly
medications.contra: Liver disease, chronic alcohol use (>3 drinks/day), existing hepatic impairment
medications.pearl: Check ALL medications for hidden acetaminophen (Percocet, Vicodin, NyQuil, Excedrin) to prevent exceeding daily max. Most common cause of acute liver failure in North America is acetaminophen overdose. Antidote: N-Acetylcysteine (NAC).

medications.name: Morphine
medications.action: Binds mu-opioid receptors in the CNS and spinal cord, inhibiting ascending pain pathways and altering the emotional response to pain. Provides moderate-severe pain relief
medications.sideEffects: Respiratory depression (most dangerous), sedation, constipation (most common), nausea, pruritus, urinary retention, hypotension
medications.contra: Respiratory depression, severe asthma/COPD, paralytic ileus, head injury (masks neuro changes), MAO inhibitor use within 14 days
medications.pearl: Monitor respiratory rate BEFORE each dose: hold if RR <12. Naloxone (Narcan) is the reversal agent for opioid-induced respiratory depression. Always assess pain BEFORE and 30 min AFTER administration. Start a bowel regimen (stool softener + stimulant laxative) prophylactically: constipation is the one side effect patients do NOT develop tolerance to.

left: Numeric Rating Scale (NRS): Patient rates pain 0-10 (0=no pain, 10=worst imaginable). Most widely used for adults and children >8 years who can self-report
Wong-Baker FACES Scale: 6 facial expressions from smiling (no hurt) to crying (hurts worst). For children ages 3-8 years and adults with communication barriers
FLACC Scale: Face, Legs, Activity, Cry, Consolability: scored 0-2 each (total 0-10). For infants, preverbal children, and cognitively impaired adults who cannot self-report
CPOT (Critical Care Pain Observation Tool): For intubated/sedated ICU patients. Assesses facial expression, body movements, muscle tension, ventilator compliance
Behavioral indicators: Grimacing, guarding, splinting, moaning, restlessness, diaphoresis, tachycardia, hypertension, decreased mobility
right: Acute Pain: Sudden onset, identifiable cause, time-limited. Associated with autonomic responses (tachycardia, hypertension, diaphoresis, pupil dilation)
Chronic Pain: Persists >3-6 months beyond expected healing time. Autonomic adaptation occurs (vital signs may be NORMAL despite severe pain). Depression and sleep disturbance common
Neuropathic Pain: Burning, tingling, shooting, electric shock quality. Results from nerve damage (diabetic neuropathy, phantom limb, postherpetic neuralgia)
Referred Pain: Felt at a location distant from the source. Examples: Cardiac pain → left arm/jaw, Gallbladder → right shoulder (Kehr sign), Appendicitis → periumbilical → RLQ (McBurney)
Breakthrough Pain: Transient flare of pain that exceeds baseline despite around-the-clock analgesic therapy. Requires PRN rescue dose

quiz.question: Which pain assessment tool is most appropriate for a 4-year-old child after surgery?
quiz.rationale: The Wong-Baker FACES Pain Scale is the most appropriate tool for children ages 3-8 years. It uses 6 cartoon faces ranging from a smiling face (no hurt) to a crying face (hurts worst). The child points to the face that best represents their pain level. Young children cannot reliably use abstract numeric scales (NRS) or visual analog scales. The McGill Pain Questionnaire is too complex for pediatric use.

pain-assessment-rpn.title: Ağrı Değerlendirme Ölçekleri (NRS, FACES)
pain-assessment-rpn.pathophysiology:
Ağrı, birden fazla sinir yolunu içeren karmaşık bir duyusal ve duygusal deneyimdir.

Nocicepsiyon dört aşamayı içerir:
1. Transdüksiyon: Zararlı uyarılar (termal, mekanik, kimyasal) periferik dokulardaki nociceptörleri (serbest sinir uçları) aktive eder, uyarıyı elektrik sinyallerine dönüştürür. İltihaplayıcı medyatörler (prostaglandinler, bradikinin, madde P, histamin) ağrı eşiğini düşürür (duyarlılık).
2. İletim: Sinyaller A-delta lifleri (hızlı, keskin, iyi lokalize olmuş ağrı) ve C lifleri (yavaş, donuk, yaygın, ağrı) aracılığıyla spinal kordun dorsal boynuna gider, ardından spinotalamik yol ile talamusa ve somatosensory kortekse yükselir.
3. Algılama: Beyin sinyali ağrı olarak yorumlar. Burada ağrı bilinçli bir deneyim haline gelir. Dikkat, duygu, geçmiş deneyim ve kültürel faktörlerden etkilenir.
4. Modülasyon: Beyin sapından inen yollar, ağrı sinyallerini inhibe edebilen veya artırabilen endorfinler, enkephalinler, serotonin ve norepinefrin salgılar (kapı kontrol teorisi).

Ağrı her zaman öznel bir deneyimdir: hastanın kendi bildirimi, ağrının en güvenilir göstergesidir. Ağrı, hastanın söylediği her şeydir, hastanın söylediği her zaman meydana gelir (Margo McCaffery).

diagnostics: Eye opening response (1-4 points)
Verbal response (1-5 points)
Motor response (1-6 points)
Total GCS score (3-15)
Pupil size and reactivity assessment
management: Report GCS ≤ 8 immediately (indicates need for airway protection)
Report any decrease of 2+ points from baseline
Monitor trends over time rather than single values
Implement safety precautions for decreased LOC
nursingActions: Assess and calculate GCS score at prescribed intervals
Document each component separately (E, V, M) plus total score
Report declining GCS immediately to RN/physician
Assess pupil size, equality, and reactivity
Report GCS ≤ 8 immediately (patient cannot protect airway)
Compare current GCS to baseline and trend over time
clinicalPearls: Always report the TOTAL score AND individual components: E4V5M6 = GCS 15. The breakdown matters more than the total: a patient with E4V1M6 (GCS 11) has a very different clinical picture than E3V4M4 (also GCS 11).
A GCS drop of ≥2 points from baseline is clinically significant and requires IMMEDIATE reassessment and provider notification.
Decorticate (flexion) is BETTER than decerebrate (extension): both are ominous, but decerebrate indicates damage at a deeper (brainstem) level with worse prognosis.
For intubated patients, document verbal component as '1T' or 'VT' and calculate the score without the verbal component (best possible = E4VTM6).

Apply painful stimuli correctly: Trapezius squeeze (pinch the trapezius muscle) or central pressure (sternal rub) for central stimulation; nail bed pressure for peripheral stimulation. Peripheral stimulus tests spinal reflexes, not brain function.

medications.name: Mannitol
medications.action: Creates an osmotic gradient that draws water from cerebral tissue into the intravascular space, reducing cerebral edema and intracranial pressure (ICP)
medications.sideEffects: Dehydration, electrolyte imbalances (hyponatremia, hypokalemia), rebound increased ICP with prolonged use, hypotension
medications.contra: Anuria, severe dehydration, active intracranial bleeding (relative: may worsen hemorrhage through osmotic expansion)
medications.pearl: Administer through a filter needle (crystals may form). Monitor serum osmolality: hold if >320 mOsm/kg (risk of renal failure). Keep the head of bed at 30° and maintain midline head position to optimize venous drainage. Monitor ICP, neuro checks, and urine output closely.

left: GCS 15: Fully alert, oriented, follows commands: normal neurological baseline
GCS 13-14: Mild impairment: confused but responsive, monitor closely q1-2h
GCS 9-12: Moderate impairment: may open eyes to pain, confused speech, localizes pain
Trend is critical: A GCS drop of ≥2 points is significant and requires immediate intervention
Motor component is the strongest predictor of outcome: document and trend separately
right: GCS ≤8: SEVERE: coma, cannot protect airway. Intubation indicated. Neurosurgical consult.
Decorticate posturing (GCS Motor 3): Arms flexed to chest, legs extended: damage ABOVE brainstem (cortical). Memory: 'cor' = core = flexion toward body core
Decerebrate posturing (GCS Motor 2): Arms and legs extended, internally rotated: damage AT brainstem level. WORSE prognosis. Memory: 'de-cerebrate' = brain is being destroyed
Unequal pupils + declining GCS: Herniation syndrome: EMERGENCY. Elevate HOB 30°, hyperventilate, mannitol
GCS cannot be accurately assessed if patient is sedated, paralyzed, or intubated (verbal component untestable: document as 'T' for tube)

quiz.question: A patient with a head injury opens eyes to pain, makes incomprehensible sounds, and demonstrates decerebrate posturing. What is the GCS score?
quiz.rationale: Eye opening to pain = E2, Incomprehensible sounds = V2, Extension/decerebrate posturing = M2. Total GCS = 2 + 2 + 2 = 6. This patient has a SEVERE traumatic brain injury (GCS ≤8) and cannot protect their airway. Immediate interventions include: Intubation for airway protection, elevate HOB 30°, maintain midline head position, neurosurgical consultation, and continuous neurological monitoring.

gcs-assessment-rpn.title: Glasgow Koması Ölçeği (GCS)
gcs-assessment-rpn.pathophysiology:
Glasgow Koması Ölçeği, bilinç düzeyini ve nörolojik fonksiyonu değerlendirmek için en yaygın kullanılan standartlaştırılmış araçtır. Üç bağımsız yanıtı değerlendirir: Göz Açma, Sözel Yanıt ve Motor Yanıt. Toplam puan 3 (derin koma/beyin ölümü) ile 15 (tamamen uyanık ve oryante) arasında değişir.

Göz Açma (E): Bilincin uyanıklık bileşenini test eder, esas olarak beyin sapındaki retiküler aktive edici sistem (RAS) tarafından yönetilir.
• 4 = Kendiliğinden (uyarı olmadan gözler açık: RAS işlevsel)
• 3 = Ses/emir ile (konuşulduğunda gözlerini açar)
• 2 = Basınç/ağrı ile (sadece ağrılı uyarana gözlerini açar)
• 1 = Yok (ağrı ile bile göz açma yok: şiddetli beyin sapı disfonksiyonu)

Sözel Yanıt (V): Bilişsel/bilinç bileşenini test eder, esas olarak serebral korteks ve dil merkezlerini (Broca ve Wernicke bölgeleri) içerir.
• 5 = Oryante (kim olduğunu, nerede olduğunu, ne zaman olduğunu ve ne olduğunu bilir)
• 4 = Kafası karışık (cümleler kurar ama oryante değildir)
• 3 = Uygunsuz kelimeler (rastgele kelimeler, sürdürülebilir bir konuşma yok)
• 2 = Anlaşılmaz sesler (inleme, inleme, kelime yok)
• 1 = Yok (sözel yanıt yok)

Motor Yanıt (M): Hem kortikal hem de beyin sapı motor yollarını test eder. Bu, klinik olarak en önemli bileşendir.
• 6 = Emirleri yerine getirir (talimatları takip eder: 'kollarını kaldır')
• 5 = Ağrıyı lokalize eder (ağrı kaynağını bulmaya çalışır)
• 4 = Geri çekilme/fleksiyon (ağrıdan uzaklaşır: spinal refleks)
• 3 = Anormal fleksiyon (dekortikasyon duruşu: kollar bükülü, bacaklar uzatılmış: kortikal hasar)
• 2 = Uzatma (dekerebrasyon duruşu: kollar ve bacaklar uzatılmış ve içe dönük: beyin sapı hasarı)
• 1 = Yok (motor yanıt yok: şiddetli beyin sapı yaralanması)

Kritik eşikler: GCS ≤8 = Şiddetli (koma: hava yolunu koruyamaz, entübasyon gereklidir), GCS 9-12 = Orta, GCS 13-15 = Hafif.

diagnostics: Sensory perception assessment (1-4 points)
Moisture exposure assessment (1-4 points)
Activity level assessment (1-4 points)
Mobility assessment (1-4 points)
Nutrition assessment (1-4 points)
Friction and shear assessment (1-3 points)
management: Reposition every 2 hours (use 30-degree lateral position)
Use pressure-redistribution mattress for high-risk patients
Optimize nutrition (high protein, vitamin C, zinc)
Manage moisture and incontinence promptly
Float heels off bed using pillow under calves
nursingActions: Perform Braden Scale assessment on admission and every shift
Implement prevention interventions based on score (lower score = higher risk)
Reposition patient every 2 hours and document
Assess skin over bony prominences each shift (sacrum, heels, occiput)
Report Braden score ≤ 12 (high risk) to RN/charge nurse
Document skin assessment findings and prevention measures implemented
clinicalPearls: Lower Braden Score = Higher risk. Remember: 'Low is Bad': a score of 9 is very high risk, while 23 is no risk.

Reposition every 2 hours: this is the single most important nursing intervention for pressure injury prevention. Use the 30-degree lateral position (not 90-degree) to avoid pressure on the trochanter.
Float heels off the bed completely: heels are the second most common site for pressure injuries (after sacrum). Use a pillow under the calves, NOT under the heels.
Assess nutritional status: Protein (albumin, prealbumin), calories, vitamin C, and zinc are essential for tissue integrity and wound healing. Malnutrition is a major modifiable risk factor.
Document Braden Scale score on admission and every shift (or per policy). Implement a care plan based on the score: interventions should escalate as the score decreases.

medications.name: Pressure-Redistribution Mattress
medications.action: Distributes body weight over a larger surface area, reducing peak interface pressure below capillary closing pressure (32 mmHg). Types include alternating pressure, low-air-loss, and air-fluidized surfaces
medications.sideEffects: Does NOT eliminate the need for repositioning: it supplements turning, not replaces it
medications.contra: Unstable spinal fractures (some types), ensure compatibility with bed frame
medications.pearl: Select surface based on risk level: Standard foam for mild risk, alternating pressure for moderate, low-air-loss for high risk, air-fluidized for very high risk or existing Stage 3-4 injuries. Still reposition every 2 hours.

left: Stage 1: Non-blanchable erythema of intact skin: redness that does NOT turn white when pressed. First visible sign. Skin may be warm, firm, or painful
Stage 2: Partial-thickness skin loss: shallow open ulcer with red-pink wound bed. May present as intact or ruptured blister. NO slough or eschar
Stage 3: Full-thickness skin loss: subcutaneous fat may be visible, but bone/tendon/muscle NOT exposed. Slough may be present. Undermining and tunneling may occur
Stage 4: Full-thickness tissue loss with EXPOSED bone, tendon, or muscle. Slough or eschar may be present. Often includes undermining and tunneling
right: Unstageable: Full-thickness loss: base of ulcer covered by slough (yellow/tan/gray) or eschar (black/brown). TRUE depth cannot be determined until slough/eschar is removed
Deep Tissue Injury (DTI): Intact skin that is purple/maroon or blood-filled blister. Indicates damage to underlying tissue from pressure/shear. May rapidly evolve to deeper stages
Risk factors: Immobility (#1 risk), malnutrition, incontinence, decreased sensation, advanced age, diabetes, vascular disease, obesity, friction/shear
Prevention is KEY: Reposition q2h, float heels off bed, nutrition optimization (protein, calories, vitamin C, zinc), moisture management, pressure-redistribution mattress

quiz.question: A patient has a Braden Scale score of 11. How should the nurse interpret this score?
options: No significant risk: standard care is adequate
High risk: implement aggressive prevention measures immediately
The score indicates an existing pressure injury
quiz.rationale: A Braden Scale score of 10-12 indicates HIGH risk for pressure injury development. The nurse should immediately implement aggressive prevention measures: reposition every 2 hours (or more frequently), use a pressure-redistribution mattress, optimize nutrition (high protein, vitamin C, zinc), manage moisture/incontinence, float heels off the bed, and conduct skin assessments every shift focusing on bony prominences (sacrum, heels, occiput, scapulae, elbows). Lower Braden scores = higher risk.

braden-scale-rpn.title: Basınç Yarası Riski için Braden Ölçeği
braden-scale-rpn.pathophysiology:
Basınç yaraları (eski adıyla 'basınç ülserleri' veya 'yatak yaraları'), yumuşak doku üzerindeki sürekli basınç, kapiller kapanma basıncını (~32 mmHg) aştığında gelişir ve bu da lokalize iskemiyi, doku hipoksisini ve hücre ölümünü tetikler. Hasar içten dışa doğru gerçekleşir: derin doku yaralanması genellikle görünür cilt değişikliklerinden önce gelir.

Braden Ölçeği, basınç yarası riskini tahmin etmek için en yaygın kullanılan kanıta dayalı araçtır. ALTIDEN alt ölçek değerlendirir, her biri 1-4 arasında puanlanır (sürtünme/kayma hariç, bu 1-3'tür):

1. Duyusal Algı (1-4): Basınca bağlı rahatsızlığa yanıt verme yeteneği. Puan 1 = tamamen sınırlı (bilinçsiz/felçli), Puan 4 = hiçbir bozulma yok.
2. Nem (1-4): Cildin nemle maruz kalma derecesi. Puan 1 = sürekli nemli, Puan 4 = nadiren nemli.
3. Aktivite (1-4): Fiziksel aktivite derecesi. Puan 1 = yatakta, Puan 4 = sık sık yürür.
4. Hareketlilik (1-4): Vücut pozisyonunu değiştirme ve kontrol etme yeteneği. Puan 1 = tamamen hareketsiz, Puan 4 = sınırlama yok.
5. Beslenme (1-4): Alışılmış gıda alım düzeni. Puan 1 = çok zayıf, Puan 4 = mükemmel.
6. Sürtünme ve Kayma (1-3): Puan 1 = sorun (yardım gerektirir, yatakta kayar), Puan 3 = belirgin bir sorun yok.

Toplam Puan: Aralık 6-23. DAHA DÜŞÜK puanlar = DAHA YÜKSEK risk.
• 15-18 = Hafif risk
• 13-14 = Orta risk
• 10-12 = Yüksek risk
• ≤9 = Çok yüksek risk

riskFactors: Cognitive impairment affecting health history accuracy
Multiple comorbidities with complex medication regimens
diagnostics: Comprehensive health history using OLDCARTS framework
Complete head-to-toe physical examination (IPPA)
Functional assessment (ADLs and IADLs)
Psychosocial and spiritual assessment
Review of systems across all body systems
Diagnostic test review and correlation with clinical findings
management: Develop nursing care plan based on assessment findings
Prioritize problems using clinical judgment (Maslow's hierarchy)
Initiate appropriate nursing interventions for identified problems
Collaborate with interdisciplinary team for complex findings
Plan for reassessment intervals based on acuity
nursingActions: Collect comprehensive subjective data (health history, ROS, social history)
Perform systematic physical examination using inspection, palpation, percussion, auscultation
Assess functional status using validated tools (Katz ADL, Lawton IADL)
Evaluate psychosocial needs (coping, support system, spiritual concerns)
Document findings completely and accurately
Identify and prioritize nursing diagnoses based on assessment data
clinicalPearls: ALWAYS start with the patient's chief complaint: what brought them here? This guides the depth and focus of your assessment even within a comprehensive framework.

Use therapeutic communication during the health history: Open-ended questions first ('Tell me about your pain'), then focused/closed-ended questions to clarify ('Is it sharp or dull?'). Avoid leading questions.

Document pertinent negatives: what you specifically looked for and did NOT find. 'Denies chest pain, SOB, palpitations' is as important as documenting positive findings for building the clinical picture.

The assessment is not a one-time event: it is continuous. Initial comprehensive assessment → ongoing focused assessments → reassessment after every intervention. Clinical status changes require reassessment.

Cultural competence: Ask about health beliefs, traditional remedies, dietary practices, religious considerations, decision-making patterns, and communication preferences. Use professional interpreters for language barriers: NEVER family members for medical interpretation.

medications.name: Assessment Documentation Tools
medications.action: Standardized methods for organizing and communicating assessment data: SBAR (Situation, Background, Assessment, Recommendation) for handoff; SOAPIE for charting; Gordon's Functional Health Patterns for holistic assessment organization
medications.sideEffects: Incomplete documentation creates liability and communication gaps: document ALL findings including pertinent negatives
medications.contra: Never chart in advance, never chart for someone else, never alter documentation after the fact
medications.pearl: If it wasn't documented, it wasn't done. Use objective, measurable language: 'Patient reports pain 7/10 in right lower abdomen, guarding on palpation' NOT 'Patient seems like they might be in pain.'

left: Subjective Data (what the patient tells you): Chief complaint, symptom description (OLDCARTS), medical history, medication list, allergies, family history, social history, review of systems, pain level, functional status

Health History Mnemonics: HPI = OLDCARTS (Onset, Location, Duration, Character, Aggravating, Relieving, Timing, Severity). Allergies = document REACTION (rash vs anaphylaxis). Social = ETOH, tobacco, drugs quantified

Review of Systems: Systematically ask about EACH body system: constitutional (weight changes, fever, fatigue), HEENT, respiratory, cardiovascular, GI, GU, musculoskeletal, neurological, psychiatric, integumentary
Functional Assessment: Katz Index (ADLs), Lawton Scale (IADLs), fall risk, mobility level, cognitive status, sensory deficits (vision, hearing)
right: Objective Data (what you observe/measure): Vital signs, physical exam findings, diagnostic results, behavior, appearance, gait, affect

Physical Exam: General survey → VS → HEENT → Neck → Chest/Lungs → Heart/Vascular → Abdomen → MSK → Neuro → Integumentary → GU (as indicated). Compare bilaterally. Document normal AND abnormal findings
Documentation: Use approved frameworks: SOAPIE (Subjective, Objective, Assessment, Plan, Intervention, Evaluation), or DAR (Data, Action, Response), or Narrative
Critical Thinking: Don't just collect data: INTERPRET it. Cluster findings, identify patterns, recognize deviations from normal, prioritize problems, and develop a plan of care

quiz.question: During a comprehensive health assessment, the nurse uses the mnemonic OLDCARTS to assess the chief complaint. What does the 'C' stand for?
quiz.rationale: OLDCARTS stands for: Onset (When did it start?), Location (Where is it?), Duration (How long does it last?), Character (What does it feel/look like?: sharp, dull, burning, throbbing, pressure), Aggravating factors (What makes it worse?), Relieving factors (What makes it better?), Timing (Is it constant or intermittent? When does it occur?), Severity (How bad is it on a 0-10 scale?). 'Character' describes the quality of the symptom in the patient's own words.

comprehensive-health-assessment.title: Kapsamlı Sağlık Değerlendirmesi
comprehensive-health-assessment.pathophysiology:
Kapsamlı bir sağlık değerlendirmesi, hem öznel hem de nesnel verilerin sistematik olarak toplanmasıdır ve hemşirelik sürecinin temelini oluşturur. Bu, odaklanmış veya vardiya değerlendirmesinin ötesine geçerek, hastanın sağlık durumunun fiziksel, psikolojik, sosyal, kültürel ve ruhsal boyutlarını içeren tam bir resmini oluşturur.

Bileşenler:

A. Sağlık Geçmişi (Öznel Veri):
1. Biyografik veriler: Yaş, cinsiyet, meslek, eğitim, medeni durum
2. Ana şikayet (CC): Bakım arama nedeni, hastanın kendi sözleriyle
3. Mevcut Hastalık Geçmişi (HPI): OLDCARTS: Başlangıç, Yer, Süre, Karakter, Kötüleştirici faktörler, Rahatlatıcı faktörler, Zamanlama, Şiddet
4. Geçmiş Tıbbi Geçmiş (PMH): Kronik hastalıklar, ameliyatlar, hastaneye yatışlar, yaralanmalar, kan transfüzyonları
5. İlaçlar: Reçeteli, OTC, bitkisel, takviyeler: dozlar, sıklık, uyum ile birlikte
6. Alerjiler: İlaç, gıda, çevresel: reaksiyon türünü belgeleyin (anaflaksi vs intolerans)
7. Aile Geçmişi (FH): Kalıtsal durumlar, birinci derece akrabalarındaki ölüm nedenleri
8. Sosyal Geçmiş (SH): Tütün, alkol, uyuşturucu, egzersiz, diyet, meslek, yaşam durumu, destek sistemi
9. Sistem İncelemesi (ROS): Tüm vücut sistemleri boyunca sistematik baştan ayağa semptom sorgulaması

B. Fiziksel Muayene (Nesnel Veri):
Muayene, palpasyon, perküsyon ve oskültasyon (IPPA) kullanarak sistematik cephalocaudal değerlendirme. Genel anket, vital bulgular ve tüm vücut sistemlerini içerir.

C. Fonksiyonel Değerlendirme:
Günlük Yaşam Aktiviteleri (ADLs): Banyo, giyinme, tuvalet, transfer, kontinans, beslenme
IADLs (Enstrümantal ADLs): Yemek pişirme, temizlik, finans yönetimi, ulaşım, ilaç yönetimi

D. Psiko-sosyal Değerlendirme:
Ruh hali, duygular, başa çıkma mekanizmaları, destek sistemleri, ruhsal ihtiyaçlar, kültürel hususlar, sağlık okuryazarlığı

riskFactors: Distracting injuries masking more critical problems
Unresponsive patient with no available history
Mass casualty situations with limited resources
diagnostics: A - Airway assessment with cervical spine protection
B - Breathing assessment (rate, depth, bilateral chest rise, SpO2)
C - Circulation assessment (pulse quality, skin color/temp, capillary refill, hemorrhage control)
D - Disability assessment (GCS, pupil response, glucose level)
E - Exposure/Environment (full body examination, temperature management)
management: Address life threats in ABCDE sequence
Activate rapid response or code team as needed
Initiate BLS/ACLS protocols as indicated
Establish IV access and cardiac monitoring
Prepare for definitive interventions (intubation, chest decompression, blood products)
nursingActions: Perform rapid primary survey using ABCDE framework
Address airway obstruction before moving to breathing assessment
Control hemorrhage with direct pressure
Assess neurological status rapidly (GCS, pupils)
Expose patient fully while preventing hypothermia
Reassess primary survey after each intervention
clinicalPearls: ABCDE is SEQUENTIAL: you must fix 'A' before assessing 'B.' A patient with an obstructed airway will die before any other problem matters. Airway is ALWAYS first.
In trauma, assume c-spine injury until cleared: use jaw thrust (NOT head-tilt chin-lift) to open the airway. Maintain manual in-line stabilization during all airway maneuvers.

The most common preventable cause of death in trauma is uncontrolled hemorrhage. Control bleeding with direct pressure first, then tourniquet for extremity hemorrhage that is not controlled by direct pressure.
Reassess ABCDE after EVERY intervention: the primary survey is not a one-time assessment. The patient's condition can change rapidly, and previously stable elements can deteriorate.

The secondary survey (complete head-to-toe) is only performed AFTER the primary survey is complete and all life threats are addressed. Never get distracted by dramatic-looking but non-life-threatening injuries.

medications.name: Tranexamic Acid (TXA)
medications.action: Inhibits plasminogen activation and fibrinolysis, stabilizing blood clots and reducing hemorrhage. Used in trauma with significant hemorrhage or risk of hemorrhage
medications.sideEffects: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, thromboembolic events (DVT, PE: rare at recommended doses), seizures at very high doses
medications.contra: Active thromboembolic disease, history of seizures (relative), subarachnoid hemorrhage (increased risk of cerebral ischemia)
medications.pearl: Administer within 3 HOURS of injury for trauma: efficacy drops significantly after 3 hours and may increase mortality if given >3 hours post-injury (CRASH-2 trial). Dose: 1g IV over 10 min, then 1g over 8 hours. Also used for postpartum hemorrhage.

left: A: Patent airway: no stridor, gurgling, or snoring. Speaking in full sentences. Trachea midline
B: Breathing: bilateral chest rise, clear lung sounds, RR 12-20, SpO2 >95%, no accessory muscle use
C: Circulation: pulse present, strong and regular, BP within normal limits, cap refill <3 sec, warm and dry skin, no active hemorrhage
D: Disability: Alert (AVPU), GCS 15, PERRLA, blood glucose normal, no lateralizing signs
E: Fully exposed and inspected: no additional injuries identified, patient kept warm
right: A: Compromised: Stridor, gurgling, inability to speak, cyanosis: IMMEDIATELY intervene: suction, position, airway adjunct, prepare for intubation
B: Compromised: Absent breath sounds, tracheal deviation, JVD, respiratory distress: consider tension pneumothorax (needle decompression), massive hemothorax (chest tube)
C: Compromised: Tachycardia, hypotension, cool/pale skin, active bleeding: control hemorrhage, 2 large-bore IVs, fluid resuscitation, prepare for transfusion
D: Compromised: GCS ≤8 (protect airway), unequal pupils (herniation), focal deficits (stroke vs mass lesion): CT scan, neurosurgical consult
E: Hypothermia: Core temp <35°C in trauma = lethal triad risk. Active warming mandatory. Log roll to check posterior: missed injuries kill

quiz.question: During the primary survey, a trauma patient has absent breath sounds on the left, tracheal deviation to the right, and jugular venous distension. What is the priority intervention?
options: Start two large-bore IVs and administer fluid bolus
Perform needle decompression on the left chest
Obtain a portable chest X-ray immediately
quiz.rationale: This patient has the classic signs of a TENSION PNEUMOTHORAX: absent breath sounds on the affected side, tracheal deviation AWAY from the affected side, JVD (due to impaired venous return from mediastinal shift), and likely hypotension. This is a life-threatening emergency in the 'B' (Breathing) step of the primary survey. Needle decompression (14-gauge needle, 2nd intercostal space, midclavicular line) is the immediate lifesaving intervention: do NOT wait for X-ray confirmation. This is a clinical diagnosis that requires immediate action.

primary-survey-rn.title: Birincil Değerlendirme (ABCDE) Değerlendirmesi
primary-survey-rn.pathophysiology:
Birincil Değerlendirme, travma ve acil durumlarda yaşamı tehdit eden durumları tanımlamak ve hemen tedavi etmek için kullanılan hızlı, sistematik bir değerlendirme yaklaşımıdır. Hayatı tehdit eden durumları öncelik sırasına göre ele alan ABCDE çerçevesini takip eder. Her adım, bir sonraki adıma geçmeden önce değerlendirilip yönetilmelidir: hava yolu tehlikeye girmişse solunumu değerlendiremezsiniz.

A: Hava Yolu (C-omurga koruması ile):
• Hava yolu açık mı? Engelleri kontrol edin (dil, kan, kusma, yabancı cisim, ödem)
• Stridor, gurgulama, horlama (kısmi obstrüksiyon belirtileri) dinleyin
• Travma şüphesi varsa: Çene itme ile C-omurga immobilizasyonunu koruyun (BAŞ EĞİMİ ÇENE KALDIRMA DEĞİL)
• Müdahale: Aspirasyon, oral/nazal hava yolu, gerektiğinde entübasyon

B: Solunum:
• Hasta nefes alıyor mu? Hız, derinlik, ritim, simetri, çaba
• Göğsü açın: Trakeal sapma, JVD, flail göğüs, açık yaralar arayın
• İki taraflı oskültasyon: nefes sesleri yok/azalmış mı?
• SpO2 izleme
• Hayatı tehdit eden durumlar: Tansiyon pneumotoraksı, açık pneumotoraks, büyük hemotoraks, flail göğüs
• Müdahale: O2, iğne dekompresyonu, göğüs bandı, entübasyon

C: Dolaşım:
• Nabız var mı? Hız, kalite, düzenlilik
• Şok belirtileri: Taşikardi, hipotansiyon, soğuk/soluk/terli cilt, gecikmiş kapiler dolum
• Kanamayı tanımlayın ve kontrol edin: Doğrudan basınç, turnike, pelvik bağlayıcı
• İki büyük çaplı IV (14-16 gauge), ılık kristaloid veya kan ürünleri
• Hayatı tehdit eden durumlar: Hemorajik şok, kalp tamponadı, büyük kanama

D: Engellilik (Nörolojik):
• AVPU: Uyanık, Sözlü yanıt, Ağrı yanıtı, Tepkisiz
• GCS (Glasgow Koması Ölçeği): Göz + Sözlü + Motor = 3-15
• Pupiller: Boyut, eşitlik, tepki (PERRLA)
• Kan şekeri (hipoglisemi nörolojik acil durumu taklit edebilir)
• Lateralize belirtiler: Tek taraflı zayıflık, eşit olmayan pupiller

E: Maruz Kalma/Çevre:
• Tüm yaraları tanımlamak için hastayı tamamen soyun (gerekirse giysileri kesin)
• Arka yüzeyleri değerlendirmek için log roll yapın
• Hipotermiyi önleyin: Sıcak battaniyeler, sıcak IV sıvılar, sıcak ortam
• Travmanın 'ölümcül üçlüsü': Hipotermi + Asidoz + Koagülopati

riskFactors: Posterior circulation stroke (may be missed by NIHSS)
Young patients with stroke risk factors
diagnostics: Level of consciousness assessment (LOC questions and commands)
Best gaze (horizontal eye movements)
Visual fields testing
Facial palsy assessment
Motor arm and leg drift testing (5 and 10 seconds)
Limb ataxia testing
Sensory assessment
Best language (naming, reading, describing)
Dysarthria assessment
Extinction and inattention (double simultaneous stimulation)
management: Calculate and document total NIHSS score (0-42)
Report NIHSS ≥ 6 as moderate-severe stroke requiring intervention
Activate stroke team for acute presentations within treatment window
Serial NIHSS assessments to detect improvement or deterioration
Coordinate with stroke team for thrombolytic eligibility assessment
nursingActions: Perform NIHSS assessment accurately using standardized script
Document each component score and total score
Perform serial assessments at prescribed intervals (q1h initially)
Report any increase of ≥ 2 points immediately (worsening stroke)
Assess dysphagia before allowing oral intake
Monitor for complications of stroke (aspiration, hemorrhagic conversion, cerebral edema)
clinicalPearls: NIHSS scoring must be precise and consistent: even small score changes guide major treatment decisions (tPA, thrombectomy). Certification training is required.
Score what the patient DOES, not what you think they CAN do. If the patient doesn't attempt the task, score it as unable. Don't coach or encourage beyond standard prompts.
Motor drift assessment: Ask patient to hold arms extended (palms up) at 90° sitting or 45° supine for 10 seconds. ANY downward drift before 10 seconds is abnormal. Test each arm separately.

The NIHSS underestimates posterior circulation strokes (vertebrobasilar): a patient can have a devastating basilar artery occlusion with a relatively low NIHSS score because ataxia, vertigo, and cranial nerve deficits are not heavily weighted.

Always correlate NIHSS with imaging. A high NIHSS with a large vessel occlusion on CTA = candidate for mechanical thrombectomy (up to 24 hours in select patients with salvageable tissue on perfusion imaging).

medications.name: Alteplase (tPA / Activase)
medications.action: Converts plasminogen to plasmin, which enzymatically degrades fibrin clots, restoring blood flow to ischemic brain tissue. Must be administered within 4.5 hours of symptom onset (3 hours for >80 years old or certain contraindications)
medications.sideEffects: Hemorrhage (most serious: intracranial hemorrhage in ~6% of patients), angioedema (orolingual swelling, especially with ACE inhibitor use), bleeding from IV sites, gums, or GI tract
medications.contra: Active internal bleeding, history of intracranial hemorrhage, recent (3 months) stroke or head trauma, intracranial neoplasm, blood glucose <50 mg/dL, platelet count <100,000, INR >1.7, systolic BP >185 or diastolic >110 (must be lowered before administration), recent major surgery
medications.pearl: Dose: 0.9 mg/kg (max 90 mg): give 10% as IV bolus over 1 min, remaining 90% as IV infusion over 60 min. BP must be maintained <180/105 during and for 24 hours after infusion. No anticoagulants or antiplatelets for 24 hours post-tPA. Perform neuro checks every 15 min during infusion, every 30 min for 6 hours, then every hour for 18 hours. Any neuro deterioration during infusion = STOP tPA immediately, stat CT to rule out hemorrhagic conversion.

left: Score 0-4 (Minor): Minimal deficits, likely lacunar stroke. May not qualify for tPA but still requires workup, monitoring, and secondary prevention
Score 5-15 (Moderate): Significant deficits: weakness, speech changes, visual field loss. Good candidate for thrombolytic therapy within window
Score 16-20 (Moderate-Severe): Major deficits with significant disability likely. Consider thrombectomy for large vessel occlusion
Score 21-42 (Severe): Devastating stroke with dense hemiplegia, global aphasia, gaze deviation. High risk of hemorrhagic transformation with tPA
right: Time is Brain: Every minute of ischemic stroke destroys approximately 1.9 million neurons. Door-to-needle time goal for tPA: ≤60 minutes
NIHSS must be performed by CERTIFIED assessors: consistency and accuracy are critical for treatment decisions and trending
Score EACH item independently: do not let findings from one category influence scoring of another. Score what you SEE, not what you think is happening
Document the EXACT time of onset (or last known normal): this determines the treatment window. If onset is unknown, use the last time the patient was seen at baseline
Reassess NIHSS: Before tPA, 2 hours after tPA, 24 hours post-treatment, at discharge, and any time there is a clinical change

quiz.question: A patient presents with sudden right-sided weakness, slurred speech, and right facial droop 90 minutes ago. NIHSS is 12. What is the priority nursing action?
options: Administer aspirin 325 mg and observe for 24 hours
Prepare for IV tPA administration: the patient is within the treatment window
Schedule an MRI for tomorrow morning to confirm diagnosis
Start heparin drip and notify the neurologist
quiz.rationale: This patient has a moderate ischemic stroke (NIHSS 12) and is within the tPA treatment window (90 min < 4.5 hours). The priority is to prepare for IV tPA administration. Time is brain: every minute of delay means more neuronal death. The nurse should: ensure BP is <185/110 (treat if needed), confirm no contraindications, obtain CBC, PT/INR, glucose, type & screen, weigh the patient for dosing (0.9 mg/kg, max 90 mg), and establish two IV lines. Aspirin is contraindicated for 24 hours after tPA. Heparin is not first-line for acute ischemic stroke. MRI delay is unacceptable when the patient qualifies for time-sensitive treatment.

nih-stroke-scale.title: NIH İnme Ölçeği (NIHSS) Değerlendirmesi
nih-stroke-scale.pathophysiology:
Ulusal Sağlık Enstitüleri İnme Ölçeği (NIHSS), inme şiddetini nicelendirerek tedavi kararlarını yönlendirmek (özellikle trombolitik uygunluk) ve hasta sonuçlarını tahmin etmek için kullanılan sistematik, doğrulanmış bir değerlendirme aracıdır. İlk sunumda, trombolitik uygulamadan önce ve sonra, ilk 24 saat içinde her 6 saatte bir ve taburcu olurken uygulanır.

NIHSS, her biri 0-2, 0-3 veya 0-4 ölçeğinde puanlanan 11 nörolojik işlev kategorisini değerlendirir, 0 = normaldir. Toplam puan 0 ile 42 arasında değişir.

1a. Bilinç Seviyesi (LOC): 0-3 (uyanık → koma)
1b. LOC Soruları: 0-2 (ay ve yaş: her ikisi doğru → hiçbiri doğru değil)
1c. LOC Komutları: 0-2 (gözleri aç/kapa, sık/serbest bırak: her ikisi doğru → hiçbiri)
2. En İyi Bakış: 0-2 (normal → zorlanmış sapma)
3. Görme Alanları: 0-3 (kayıp yok → iki taraflı hemianopi/körlük)
4. Yüz Felci: 0-3 (normal → tam felç iki taraflı)
5a/5b. Motor Kol (L/R): 0-4 (kayma yok → hareket yok)
6a/6b. Motor Bacak (L/R): 0-4 (kayma yok → hareket yok)
7. Uzuv Ataksisi: 0-2 (yok → 2 uzuvda mevcut)
8. Duyusal: 0-2 (normal → şiddetli/tam kayıp)
9. En İyi Dil: 0-3 (afazi yok → sessiz/global afazi)
10. Disartri: 0-2 (normal → anlaşılmaz/anarthric)
11. Yok Olma/Dikkatsizlik: 0-2 (anormallik yok → derin ihmal)

Puan yorumlama:
• 0 = İnme belirtileri yok
• 1-4 = Küçük inme
• 5-15 = Orta inme
• 16-20 = Orta-şiddetli inme
• 21-42 = Şiddetli inme

tPA uygunluğu: Genel olarak NIHSS ≥4 için tedavi penceresi içinde (IV tPA için başlangıçtan itibaren 4.5 saate kadar) dikkate alınır. NIHSS >25, artmış hemorajik dönüşüm riski taşıyabilir.

diagnostics: qSOFA screening (RR ≥ 22, altered mentation, SBP ≤ 100)
SIRS criteria (temp >38 or <36, HR >90, RR >20, WBC >12K or <4K)
Serum lactate level (≥ 2 mmol/L indicates tissue hypoperfusion)
Blood cultures (2 sets from different sites before antibiotics)
CBC with differential, BMP, coagulation studies
Procalcitonin level for bacterial infection differentiation
management: Initiate Hour-1 Sepsis Bundle: measure lactate, blood cultures, broad-spectrum antibiotics, 30 mL/kg crystalloid if hypotensive or lactate ≥ 4
Reassess volume status and tissue perfusion after initial fluid bolus
Initiate vasopressors if hypotension persists after fluid resuscitation (target MAP ≥ 65)
De-escalate antibiotics within 48-72 hours based on culture results
nursingActions: Screen all patients with suspected infection using qSOFA/SIRS criteria
Obtain blood cultures BEFORE antibiotic administration (but do not delay antibiotics)
Administer antibiotics within 1 hour of sepsis recognition
Initiate fluid resuscitation (30 mL/kg crystalloid) and monitor response
Monitor lactate levels and lactate clearance
Assess for end-organ dysfunction (urine output, mental status, perfusion)
clinicalPearls: Hour-1 Bundle is critical: Measure lactate, blood cultures, broad-spectrum antibiotics, begin crystalloid 30 mL/kg if hypotensive or lactate ≥4. Every hour of delay increases mortality.
Blood cultures BEFORE antibiotics: but NEVER delay antibiotics waiting for cultures. If cultures cannot be obtained within minutes, give antibiotics and draw cultures when possible.

Lactate is the most important biomarker for sepsis severity and treatment response. Repeat lactate within 2-4 hours if initial value is elevated. Lactate clearance >10% in 6 hours is associated with improved survival.

Fluid responsiveness: Not all septic patients respond to fluids. Assess for fluid responsiveness using passive leg raise test, pulse pressure variation, or stroke volume variation BEFORE giving additional fluid boluses.

Sepsis can present atypically in elderly (hypothermia instead of fever, confusion without fever), neonates (temperature instability, poor feeding, lethargy), and immunocompromised patients (blunted inflammatory response). Maintain a HIGH index of suspicion in vulnerable populations.

medications.name: Norepinephrine (Levophed)
medications.action: Potent alpha-1 agonist with moderate beta-1 activity. Causes peripheral vasoconstriction (raises SVR) and mild increase in cardiac output, raising MAP to target ≥65 mmHg
medications.sideEffects: Tissue ischemia and necrosis with extravasation (administer through CENTRAL line), arrhythmias, reflex bradycardia, peripheral vasoconstriction (cool extremities)
medications.contra: Hypovolemia (correct volume deficit first), mesenteric/peripheral vascular thrombosis (relative)
medications.pearl: First-line vasopressor for septic shock per Surviving Sepsis Campaign. Start AFTER adequate fluid resuscitation (30 mL/kg). Administer via CENTRAL LINE: extravasation causes severe tissue necrosis. If extravasation occurs, infiltrate area with phentolamine. Titrate to MAP ≥65 mmHg. Monitor urine output, lactate clearance, and end-organ perfusion. If MAP not achieved, add vasopressin as second agent.

medications.name: Broad-Spectrum Antibiotics
medications.action: Empiric coverage targeting the most likely pathogens based on suspected source of infection. Administered WITHIN 1 HOUR of sepsis recognition: every hour of delay in antibiotics increases mortality by approximately 7.6%
medications.sideEffects: Drug-specific side effects, allergic reactions, C. difficile risk with prolonged broad-spectrum use, antibiotic resistance with inappropriate use
medications.contra: Known allergy (use alternative agents), de-escalate to targeted therapy once culture results are available
medications.pearl: OBTAIN BLOOD CULTURES (at least 2 sets from different sites) BEFORE administering antibiotics: but do NOT delay antibiotics if cultures take time. The 1-hour antibiotic window is more important than waiting for culture collection if it causes delay. De-escalate from broad-spectrum to targeted therapy within 48-72 hours based on culture and sensitivity results.

left: SIRS Criteria (≥2): Temp >38°C or <36°C, HR >90, RR >20 or PaCO2 <32, WBC >12K or <4K or >10% bands
qSOFA (≥2): RR ≥22, altered mentation, SBP ≤100: bedside tool, no labs needed
Early sepsis signs: Tachycardia, tachypnea, fever or hypothermia, confusion/agitation, warm flushed skin (warm shock: vasodilation phase), decreased urine output
Lactate: Early marker of tissue hypoperfusion. Normal <2 mmol/L. Elevated lactate in suspected infection = RED FLAG for sepsis
right: Late/decompensated signs: Hypotension, cool/mottled/cyanotic skin (cold shock: vasoconstriction phase), oliguria, obtundation, DIC (petechiae, oozing)
Organ dysfunction indicators: PaO2/FiO2 <300 (lungs), platelets <100K (coag), bilirubin >2 (liver), creatinine >2 (kidneys), GCS <15 (CNS), MAP <70 (cardiovascular)
Septic Shock: MAP <65 despite fluids + vasopressors + lactate >2 mmol/L. Mortality 40-60%
Nursing priority: Recognize early, obtain cultures BEFORE antibiotics, administer antibiotics within 1 HOUR of recognition, aggressive fluid resuscitation (30 mL/kg crystalloid)

quiz.question: A nurse notes the following in a patient with a UTI: RR 24, heart rate 110, BP 88/54, temperature 38.9°C, WBC 18,000, and confusion. The patient's baseline mental status was alert and oriented. What is the priority nursing action?
options: Administer acetaminophen for fever reduction and reassess in 1 hour
Obtain blood cultures and prepare for immediate broad-spectrum antibiotic administration
Apply oxygen via nasal cannula and elevate the head of bed
Document findings and notify the provider at next scheduled rounding
quiz.rationale: This patient meets SIRS criteria (fever, tachycardia, tachypnea, elevated WBC) AND qSOFA criteria (RR ≥22, SBP ≤100, altered mentation) in the setting of a known infection (UTI). This is SEPSIS with concerning features for septic shock (hypotension + altered mental status). The priority is to initiate the Sepsis Hour-1 Bundle: Obtain blood cultures (at least 2 sets) and administer broad-spectrum antibiotics WITHIN 1 HOUR. Simultaneously: measure serum lactate, begin 30 mL/kg crystalloid bolus for hypotension. This is time-critical: every hour of delay increases mortality. Waiting for rounds or treating only the fever are unacceptable delays.

sepsis-screening-rn.title: Sepsis Tarama (qSOFA, SIRS Kriterleri)
sepsis-screening-rn.pathophysiology:
Sepsis, enfeksiyona karşı düzensiz bir konak yanıtı nedeniyle meydana gelen yaşamı tehdit eden organ disfonksiyonudur. Bağışıklık sisteminin aşırı aktivasyonu, iltihabi medyatörlerin (TNF-α, IL-1, IL-6) bir zincirleme reaksiyonunu, endotel hasarını, mikrodamar trombozunu ve doku hipoperfüzyonunu tetikler ve bu da ilerleyici organ yetmezliğine yol açar.

Tarama Araçları:

1. SIRS Kriterleri (Sistemik İltihabi Yanıt Sendromu): Aşağıdakilerden ≥2:
• Sıcaklık >38°C (100.4°F) VEYA <36°C (96.8°F)
• Kalp hızı >90 bpm
• Solunum hızı >20/dk VEYA PaCO2 <32 mmHg
• WBC >12,000 VEYA <4,000 VEYA >%10 bant (olgunlaşmamış nötrofiller)
SIRS HASSAS ama ÖZEL DEĞİLDİR: birçok durum enfeksiyon olmadan SIRS'i tetikler.

2. qSOFA (Hızlı Sıralı Organ Yetmezliği Değerlendirmesi): Aşağıdakilerden ≥2:
• Solunum hızı ≥22/dk
• Değişmiş mentasyon (temelden herhangi bir değişiklik)
• Sistolik kan basıncı ≤100 mmHg
qSOFA, laboratuvar çalışması gerektirmeyen bir yan yatak tarama aracıdır. Puan ≥2, tam bir sepsis incelemesi ve SOFA hesaplaması başlatmalıdır.

3. SOFA Puanı (Sıralı Organ Yetmezliği Değerlendirmesi): 6 organ sistemini (solunum, koagülasyon, karaciğer, kardiyovasküler, CNS, renal) 0-4 puanla değerlendirir. Bilinen/şüpheli enfeksiyon durumunda, temel seviyeden ≥2 puanlık ani bir artış = Sepsis.

4. Septik Şok: Sepsis ARTIK MAP'yi ≥65 mmHg'de tutmak için vazopresör gereksinimi VE serum laktat >2 mmol/L, yeterli hacim resüsitasyonu olmasına rağmen.

SEP-1 paketi (CMS Temel Ölçütü), sepsis tanısı konduktan sonraki 3 saat içinde gerektirir:
• Laktat ölçümü
• Antibiyotiklerden ÖNCE kan kültürleri alın
• Geniş spektrumlu antibiyotikler uygulayın
• Hipotansiyon veya laktat ≥4 için 30 mL/kg kristaloid başlatın

6 saat içinde: Hacim durumunu yeniden değerlendirin, ilk laktat yüksekse tekrarlayın, sıvılardan sonra hipotansiyon devam ederse vazopresörler kullanın.

riskFactors: Complex presentations with multiple overlapping symptoms
Patient unable to provide reliable history
Language or cultural barriers limiting history quality
Atypical presentations in elderly or immunocompromised
diagnostics: Order targeted diagnostics based on HPI findings and differential
Order imaging guided by symptom localization and red flags
Order laboratory studies to rule in/out differential diagnoses
Order point-of-care testing for acute presentations
Order specialty consultations based on assessment findings
management: Develop differential diagnosis list ranked by probability and severity
Prescribe empiric treatment while awaiting diagnostic results if indicated
Order urgent workup for red-flag symptoms
Develop evidence-based treatment plan addressing root cause
Schedule follow-up based on clinical severity and diagnostic timeline
nursingActions: Obtain comprehensive HPI using OLDCARTS framework with detailed characterization
Elicit pertinent positives AND pertinent negatives for each differential
Perform complete review of systems to identify associated symptoms
Correlate physical examination findings with HPI data
Document clinical reasoning linking HPI to differential diagnoses
Identify red-flag symptoms requiring emergent evaluation
clinicalPearls: The HPI is the most diagnostically valuable component: a thorough HPI correctly suggests the diagnosis 75-80% of the time before you even examine the patient.

Always ask: 'What do you think is going on?' and 'What are you most worried about?': the patient's illness experience and concerns are essential for patient-centered care and may reveal critical information.

Document your clinical reasoning: Why did you order this test? Why did you choose this treatment? What diagnosis are you ruling out? This protects you legally and demonstrates medical decision-making complexity.

Red flag rule: For every chief complaint, have a mental list of 'cannot miss' diagnoses. Chest pain? Rule out MI, PE, aortic dissection, tamponade. Headache? Rule out SAH, meningitis, mass lesion, temporal arteritis.

The assessment is where you demonstrate advanced thinking: Don't just list problems: synthesize the data into a coherent clinical picture, generate a ranked differential, and articulate your reasoning for the working diagnosis and plan.

medications.name: Clinical Decision Support Tools
medications.action: Evidence-based tools that assist NPs in clinical reasoning: Wells Criteria (PE/DVT probability), HEART Score (chest pain risk stratification), CHA₂DS₂-VASc (stroke risk in AFib), CURB-65 (pneumonia severity), Ottawa Ankle/Knee Rules (imaging necessity), PHQ-9 (depression screening), GAD-7 (anxiety screening)
medications.sideEffects: Over-reliance on scoring tools without clinical judgment can miss atypical presentations. Tools are adjuncts to clinical reasoning, not replacements
medications.contra: Never use a clinical decision tool outside its validated population. Most tools were developed in specific demographics and may not generalize perfectly
medications.pearl: Know the sensitivity and specificity of your clinical tools. A highly sensitive tool (like PERC for PE) is best for ruling OUT a diagnosis (SnNOut: Sensitive test, Negative result rules Out). A highly specific tool is best for ruling IN a diagnosis (SpPIn: Specific test, Positive result rules In).

left: HPI Mastery: The HPI should read as a clinical narrative that paints a vivid picture. A well-written HPI can convey 80% of the diagnosis before the physical exam
Differential Diagnosis Process: Generate list → Rank by probability AND severity → Identify 'cannot miss' diagnoses → Order diagnostics to rule in/rule out systematically

Red Flags to Never Miss: Chest pain (ACS, PE, aortic dissection, tamponade), worst headache of life (SAH), new focal neuro deficits (stroke), fever with neutropenia (sepsis), acute abdomen (surgical emergency)
Clinical Reasoning: Bayesian thinking: prior probability × likelihood ratio = posterior probability. Pre-test probability determines whether a test is useful
right: E&M Coding: Documentation level determines billing. Comprehensive H&P = highest complexity (5+ HPI elements, 10+ ROS systems, complete PFSH, comprehensive exam, high complexity MDM)
Documentation Standards: Must support medical necessity for all diagnostics and treatments ordered. Pertinent negatives are essential for differential exclusion
Legal Considerations: The H&P is a medical-legal document. It must be accurate, timely, legible, and complete. Omissions can be interpreted as 'not performed'
Collaborative Practice: Know your state/provincial scope of practice. Some jurisdictions require collaborative agreements with physicians. Document all consultations and referrals
Evidence-Based Practice: Integrate clinical expertise + patient preferences + best available research evidence into every clinical decision

quiz.question: An NP is evaluating a 55-year-old male presenting with 2 hours of substernal chest pressure radiating to the left jaw, diaphoresis, and nausea. He took 3 nitroglycerin tabs without relief. What is the MOST critical 'cannot miss' diagnosis to evaluate first?
quiz.rationale: This presentation has multiple red flags for Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS): substernal chest pressure, jaw radiation, diaphoresis, nausea, and failure to respond to nitroglycerin. ACS is the 'cannot miss' diagnosis because delayed recognition is fatal. Immediate actions: 12-lead ECG within 10 minutes, troponin, CBC, BMP, PT/INR, aspirin 325 mg (chew), activate cath lab if STEMI. While GERD, costochondritis, and panic attacks can cause chest pain, they are diagnoses of EXCLUSION: ACS must be ruled out first because the consequences of missing it are catastrophic.

comprehensive-hpi-np.title: Kapsamlı Tarih ve Fizik
comprehensive-hpi-np.pathophysiology:
Kapsamlı tarih ve fizik muayenesi (H&P), ileri düzey uygulama hemşireliğinin temel taşını oluşturur. RN'ler tarafından yapılan odaklanmış değerlendirmelerin aksine, ileri düzey H&P, otonom klinik karar verme, diferansiyel tanı oluşturma ve tıbbi tanı ve tedavi planını belirlemek için öznel ve nesnel verilerin entegrasyonunu içerir.

H&P, hemşirelik entegrasyonu ile geleneksel tıbbi modeli takip eder:

1. Başlıca Şikayet (CC): Hastanın kendi sözleriyle. Örnek: '3 saattir göğüs ağrım var.'

2. Mevcut Hastalık Tarihi (HPI): En kritik bileşen. OLDCARTS kullanır ve genişletir:
• Başlangıç: Ani mi yoksa yavaş mı, hasta ne yapıyordu?
• Yer: Kesin anatomik yer, yayılma deseni
• Süre: Sürekli mi yoksa aralıklı mı, toplam süre, epizod uzunluğu
• Karakter: Hastanın kalite tanımı (keskin, cansız, baskı, yanma, yırtılma)
• İlişkili semptomlar: Başka ne oldu? (bulantı, terleme, nefes darlığı, ateş)
• Kötüleştirici/rahatlatıcı faktörler: Pozisyon, aktivite, yiyecek, denenen ilaçlar
• Zamanlama: Günün saati, yemek/aktivite/uyku ile ilişkisi
• Şiddet: 0-10 ölçeği, işlev üzerindeki etkisi, önceki epizodlarla karşılaştırma
• Bağlam: Hasta neyin yanlış olduğunu düşünüyor? En çok ne konusunda endişeliler?

3. Geçmiş Tıbbi Tarih: Tüm kronik durumlar, cerrahi geçmiş (tarihler, komplikasyonlar), hastaneye yatışlar, travma, kan transfüzyonları, psikiyatrik geçmiş, obstetrik geçmiş (GTPAL)

4. İlaçlar: Dozlar, yollar, sıklık, uyum, son değişiklikler, OTC, bitkisel, takviyeler, eğlence amaçlı maddeler ile birlikte tam liste

5. Alerjiler: İlaç, gıda, çevresel, lateks. ÖZEL REAKSİYONU BELGELEYİN (anafilaksi vs GI rahatsızlığı vs döküntü: bu klinik karar verme sürecini belirler)

6. Aile Geçmişi: İdeal olarak üç nesil genogram. Birinci derece akrabalar: durumlar ve başlangıç/ölüm yaşı. Aşağıdakileri tarayın: kardiyovasküler hastalık, diyabet, kanser, mental hastalık, otoimmün, genetik bozukluklar

7. Sosyal Tarih: Meslek (maruziyetler), yaşam durumu, ilişkiler, eğitim seviyesi, sağlık okuryazarlığı, egzersiz, diyet, tütün (paket-yıl), alkol (CAGE/AUDIT), yasadışı ilaçlar, cinsel tarih (partnerler, uygulamalar, koruma, STI geçmişi), aile içi şiddet taraması, ateşli silahlara erişim dahil olmak üzere genişletilmiştir.

8. Sistemlerin Gözden Geçirilmesi (ROS): 14 sistem kapsamlı inceleme. Hem olumlu hem de ilgili olumsuz yanıtları belgeleyin.

9. Fizik Muayene: Gelişmiş tekniklerle tam sistematik muayene (oftalmoskopi, otoskopi, spekulum/bimanuel muayene gerektiğinde)

10. Değerlendirme/Diferansiyel Tanı: Olası tanıların olasılığa göre sıralandığı liste. 'Atlanamaz' tanıları (dışlanması gereken en kötü senaryolar) dahil edin.

11. Plan: Tanısal testler, tedavi, eğitim, takip, yönlendirmeler

riskFactors: Premature closure (accepting diagnosis before considering alternatives)
Atypical presentations in elderly, pediatric, or immunocompromised patients
diagnostics: Order targeted laboratory studies to differentiate between competing diagnoses
Order imaging studies based on clinical probability
Order tissue sampling or biopsy when indicated for definitive diagnosis
Apply clinical decision rules (Wells, Ottawa, PERC) to guide workup
Order genetic or molecular testing when hereditary conditions suspected
management: Rank differential diagnoses by probability AND worst-case scenario (don't miss diagnoses)
Prescribe empiric treatment for most likely diagnosis while ruling out dangerous alternatives
Apply evidence-based clinical pathways for common presentations
Initiate time-sensitive treatments before all results return (sepsis, MI, stroke)
Reassess and narrow differential as diagnostic data returns
nursingActions: Generate comprehensive differential diagnosis for each chief complaint
Apply systematic approach: most common, most dangerous, most treatable
Use clinical decision rules to risk-stratify patients
Correlate history, exam, and diagnostic findings to refine differential
Document clinical reasoning and decision-making process
Identify cognitive biases and apply debiasing strategies
clinicalPearls: Always ask: 'What is the worst thing this could be?' before asking 'What is the most likely thing this is?' Missing a common diagnosis causes morbidity; missing a dangerous diagnosis causes mortality.
The problem representation is the single most important step: if you frame the case correctly, the differential generates itself. Practice summarizing cases in one sentence.

Cognitive debiasing strategies: Use diagnostic checklists, consider the opposite hypothesis, ask 'What else could this be?', perform a diagnostic timeout, and always revisit the differential when treatment isn't working.

Test ordering should be purposeful: Ask 'How will this result change my management?' before ordering any test. If the result won't change what you do, the test is not indicated (and wastes resources and may lead to false positives).
When the diagnosis is unclear, treat the most dangerous possibility while continuing the workup. You can always de-escalate treatment; you cannot undo a missed life-threatening diagnosis.

medications.name: Bayesian Reasoning Framework
medications.action: Pre-test probability × Likelihood ratio = Post-test probability. Pre-test probability is determined by prevalence, clinical features, and risk factors. Likelihood ratio (+LR and -LR) is determined by test characteristics (sensitivity and specificity). A positive test with high +LR significantly increases disease probability; a negative test with low -LR significantly decreases it
medications.sideEffects: Requires understanding of biostatistics and test characteristics: misapplication leads to diagnostic errors
medications.contra: Should not replace clinical judgment when presentation is atypical or test results are discordant with clinical picture
medications.pearl: +LR >10 or -LR <0.1 = strong test that significantly changes probability. +LR 5-10 or -LR 0.1-0.2 = moderate. +LR 2-5 or -LR 0.2-0.5 = small change. +LR 1 = useless test. Example: D-dimer for PE has excellent -LR (good for ruling OUT when pre-test probability is low) but poor +LR (cannot rule IN PE).

left: Problem Representation: One-sentence summary capturing age, sex, risk factors, acuity, and key features. This frames your differential generation

Illness Scripts: Mental templates for each disease: epidemiology (who gets it?), pathophysiology (why?), clinical features (what does it look like?), time course (how does it progress?), diagnostics (how do you confirm?)

Prioritization Matrix: Rank differentials by BOTH probability (likely) and severity (dangerous). A low-probability but life-threatening diagnosis must be ruled out before a high-probability benign one
Diagnostic Testing Strategy: Choose tests based on pre-test probability. High pre-test probability → confirmatory test (high specificity). Low pre-test probability → screening test (high sensitivity)
right: Chest Pain: MI, PE, aortic dissection, tension pneumothorax, cardiac tamponade, esophageal rupture
Headache: SAH, meningitis, temporal arteritis, intracranial mass, cavernous sinus thrombosis
Abdominal Pain: Ruptured AAA, mesenteric ischemia, ectopic pregnancy (reproductive age), appendicitis, bowel obstruction
Dyspnea: PE, tension pneumothorax, anaphylaxis, cardiac tamponade, acute HF exacerbation
Syncope: Cardiac arrhythmia, PE, aortic stenosis, ruptured AAA, ectopic pregnancy

quiz.question: A 28-year-old female presents with acute right lower quadrant abdominal pain, nausea, and a positive pregnancy test. Which 'cannot miss' diagnosis must be evaluated FIRST?
quiz.rationale: In a reproductive-age female with abdominal pain and a positive pregnancy test, ruptured ectopic pregnancy is the highest-priority 'cannot miss' diagnosis because it can cause rapid hemorrhagic shock and death. While appendicitis and ovarian torsion are also surgical emergencies on the differential, the positive pregnancy test makes ectopic pregnancy the most immediately life-threatening possibility. Immediate actions: Quantitative beta-hCG, transvaginal ultrasound to confirm intrauterine pregnancy (IUP) or identify ectopic mass, type and crossmatch, two large-bore IVs. If hemodynamically unstable with no IUP on ultrasound, emergent surgical consultation for presumed ruptured ectopic.

differential-diagnosis-np.title: Diferansiyel Tanı Oluşturma
differential-diagnosis-np.pathophysiology:
Diferansiyel tanı, benzer klinik özelliklere sahip hastalıklar veya durumlar arasında ayırt etme sürecidir. İleri düzey uygulama hemşireliğinin belirgin özelliğidir ve patofizyoloji, epidemiyoloji, klinik sunum kalıpları ve tanısal test bilgilerini entegre etmeyi gerektirir.

Tanısal Akıl Yürütme Süreci:

1. Kalıp Tanıma (Sistem 1 Düşüncesi): Deneyime dayalı hızlı, sezgisel tanıma. 'Bu zatürre gibi görünüyor.' Hızlı ama bilişsel yanlılığa eğilimli.

2. Analitik Akıl Yürütme (Sistem 2 Düşüncesi): Kasıtlı, sistematik hipotez test etme. Oluştur → Test et → Geliştir. Daha yavaş ama karmaşık veya alışılmadık sunumlar için daha doğru.

3. İkili Süreç Modeli: Uzman klinisyenler HER İKİSİNİ de kullanır: kalıp tanıma ilk hipotezleri tetikler, ardından analitik akıl yürütme bunları test eder ve geliştirir.

Diferansiyel Tanı Adımları:
1. Tam klinik verileri toplayın (H&P)
2. Problem temsilini tanımlayın: Vakayı bir cümle ile özetleyin. Örnek: 'Acil başlangıçlı pleritik göğüs ağrısı, dispne ve tek taraflı bacak şişliği olan 65 yaşında erkek sigara içen.'
3. İlk diferansiyel liste oluşturun (geniş)
4. Hastalık senaryolarını uygulayın: Hastanın sunumunu bilinen hastalık kalıplarıyla karşılaştırın
5. Olasılığa ve şiddete göre önceliklendirin (atlanamaz tanılar)
6. Tanısal testleri seçin ve yorumlayın (ön test olasılığını, duyarlılığı, özgüllüğü anlayın)
7. Sonuçlara göre diferansiyeli daraltın
8. Çalışma tanısına ulaşın ve tedaviye başlayın
9. Yanıtı izleyin: tedavi başarısız olursa, diferansiyeli yeniden değerlendirin

Yaygın Bilişsel Yanlılıklar:
• Sabitleme: Çelişkili kanıtlara rağmen ilk tanıya kilitlenme
• Erişilebilirlik: Son zamanlarda gördüğünüz tanıları aşırı değerlendirme
• Acele Kapatma: Bir tanıyı tamamen doğrulamadan kabul etme
• Doğrulama Yanlılığı: Hipotezinizi destekleyen bilgileri ararken çelişkili verileri göz ardı etme
• Tanı Momentum: Bir tanı (başka bir sağlayıcı tarafından) atandığında, alternatifleri düşünmek zorlaşır.

diagnostics: Order and interpret 12-lead ECG with systematic analysis
Order serial ECGs for evolving presentations (q15-30min for ACS)
Order continuous telemetry with ST-segment monitoring
Order electrolyte panel correlating with ECG findings (K+, Mg2+, Ca2+)
Order echocardiography for structural correlation
Order drug levels when toxicity suspected
management: Prescribe antiarrhythmic therapy based on rhythm diagnosis
Prescribe anticoagulation for atrial fibrillation using CHA2DS2-VASc scoring
Order emergent reperfusion for STEMI (activate cath lab)
Prescribe electrolyte replacement for ECG-altering imbalances
Order pacing for symptomatic bradycardia or high-degree blocks
Discontinue QT-prolonging medications when QTc > 500 ms
nursingActions: Perform systematic ECG interpretation: rate, rhythm, axis, intervals, ST/T changes
Identify STEMI patterns by coronary artery territory
Recognize lethal arrhythmias requiring immediate intervention
Correlate ECG changes with electrolyte values and medication effects
Document ECG interpretation with clinical correlation
Calculate QTc and identify risk for Torsades de Pointes
clinicalPearls: Systematic interpretation prevents missed findings: use the same 12-step method EVERY time. Speed comes with practice, not by skipping steps.
Always compare with prior ECGs when available: a new finding is more clinically significant than a chronic one. 'New LBBB + chest pain = STEMI until proven otherwise.'

Reciprocal changes CONFIRM a STEMI: if you see ST elevation in inferior leads (II, III, aVF), look for reciprocal ST depression in lateral leads (I, aVL). Reciprocal changes increase specificity for acute MI.

In posterior MI, the standard 12-lead may only show ST depression in V1-V3 (mirror image of posterior ST elevation). If suspected, obtain posterior leads (V7-V9) for direct confirmation. Always obtain right-sided leads (V4R) for inferior STEMI to assess RV involvement.

Drug-induced QT prolongation is one of the most common medication safety issues. Common offenders: Haloperidol, ondansetron, fluoroquinolones, macrolides, methadone, antipsychotics, Class IA/III antiarrhythmics. Check QTc before starting any of these and correct K+/Mg2+ first.

medications.name: Amiodarone
medications.action: Blocks potassium channels (primary), sodium channels, calcium channels, and beta-adrenergic receptors. Prolongs action potential duration and refractory period in all cardiac tissue. Used for ventricular and atrial arrhythmias
medications.sideEffects: Pulmonary toxicity (fibrosis: baseline PFTs and CXR), thyroid dysfunction (hypo/hyperthyroidism: contains iodine), hepatotoxicity, corneal microdeposits, blue-gray skin discoloration, peripheral neuropathy, photosensitivity
medications.contra: Severe sinus node disease, 2nd/3rd degree heart block (without pacemaker), cardiogenic shock, iodine hypersensitivity
medications.pearl: Half-life is 40-55 DAYS: takes weeks to reach steady state and weeks to clear. Interacts with MANY drugs (increases digoxin and warfarin levels significantly). Monitor: TFTs q6 months, LFTs q6 months, PFTs annually, ophthalmologic exam annually. Despite its side effect profile, it remains the most effective antiarrhythmic for life-threatening ventricular arrhythmias.

left: Normal Sinus Rhythm: Rate 60-100, regular, P before every QRS, PR 0.12-0.20, QRS <0.12, normal axis
STEMI: ST elevation in contiguous leads with reciprocal ST depression. Identify territory → culprit artery → activate cath lab
NSTEMI: ST depression and/or T-wave inversion WITHOUT ST elevation. Troponin positive. Medical management initially, possible cath
Bundle Branch Blocks: RBBB = 'M' pattern in V1 (rSR'), wide S in I/V6. LBBB = broad notched R in I/V6, deep S in V1. NEW LBBB with chest pain = treat as STEMI
right: Hyperkalemia: Peaked T waves → widened QRS → sine wave → asystole. K+ >6.5 is an EMERGENCY
Atrial Fibrillation: Irregularly irregular, no P waves, fibrillatory baseline. Assess rate control and stroke risk (CHA₂DS₂-VASc)

Heart Blocks: 1st degree (prolonged PR >0.20), 2nd degree Type I Wenckebach (progressive PR prolongation then dropped QRS), 2nd degree Type II (constant PR then suddenly dropped QRS: dangerous), 3rd degree complete (P and QRS dissociated: pacemaker needed)
Prolonged QT: QTc >500 ms = high risk for Torsades de Pointes. Review medications (many drugs prolong QT). Avoid hypokalemia/hypomagnesemia

quiz.question: A 12-lead ECG shows ST elevation in leads II, III, and aVF with reciprocal ST depression in leads I and aVL. Which coronary artery is most likely occluded?
quiz.rationale: ST elevation in leads II, III, and aVF with reciprocal changes in I and aVL indicates an INFERIOR STEMI. The inferior wall of the heart is supplied by the Right Coronary Artery (RCA) in approximately 85% of patients (right-dominant circulation). In the remaining 15% with left-dominant circulation, the inferior wall is supplied by the left circumflex. Immediate actions: Activate cath lab (door-to-balloon <90 min), obtain right-sided leads (V4R) to assess for RV infarction, aspirin, heparin, nitro (AVOID if RV involvement or SBP <90), and prepare for PCI. RV infarction is volume-dependent: avoid nitrates and diuretics; give IV fluids to maintain preload.

ecg-advanced-np.title: 12-Derlik EKG: Gelişmiş Yorumlama
ecg-advanced-np.pathophysiology:
Gelişmiş 12-derlik EKG yorumlama, kalp iletim sistemini hücresel düzeyde anlamayı ve elektriksel kalıpları kalbin anatomik bölgeleri ile ilişkilendirmeyi gerektirir.

Kalp İletim Yolu:
SA Düğümü (içsel hız 60-100) → Atriyal depolarizasyon (P dalgası) → AV Düğümü (0.12-0.20 sn gecikme = PR aralığı) → His Demeti → Sağ ve Sol Dalcıklar → Purkinje Lifleri → Ventiküler depolarizasyon (QRS kompleksi, <0.12 sn) → Ventiküler repolarizasyon (T dalgası)
